channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Millions of people celebrate Holi, the Hindu festival of colors
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Millions of people in South Asia celebrated Holi, the Hindu festival of colors, on Friday by smearing each other with brightly colored powder, dancing to festive music and feasting on traditional sweets prepared for the occasion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran increasingly electronically surveils women looking for headscarf violations, UN report warns
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — Iran increasingly relies on electronic surveillance and the public to inform on women refusing to wear the country's mandatory headscarf in public, even as hard-liners push for harsher penalties for those protesting the law, a United Nations report released Friday found.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine ex-President Duterte set to appear in Hague courtroom to face 'war on drugs' charges
item.description: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte is scheduled to make his first appearance before judges of the International Criminal Court.
item.dc:creator: Mike Corder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Europe's military personnel shortfalls exposed as Trump warns US security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In the year after Russia launched outright war on Ukraine, NATO leaders approved a set of military plans designed to repel an invasion of Europe. It was the biggest shake-up of the alliance’s defense readiness preparations since the Cold War.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope receives cake and glad tidings on the 12th anniversary of his papacy, marked in the hospital
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis marked the 12th anniversary of his pontificate Thursday in the hospital — the first pope to do so in modern church history — as he received increasingly positive medical news on his recovery, a cake and hundreds of messages wishing him well.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Naples struck by 4.4-magnitude quake causing minor damage and light injuries
item.description: ROME (AP) — The southern Italian city of Naples was struck by a 4.4-magnitude quake early Thursday that caused only minor damage and sent 11 people to the hospital, the most serious suffering contusions after part of a ceiling collapsed, officials said.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Johns Hopkins University says it is cutting more than 2,000 workers due to cut to federal aid
item.description: Johns Hopkins University said Thursday that it is cutting over 2,000 workers because of a federal funding cut. “The termination of more than $800 million in [U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Mexicans searching for missing relatives uncover possible mass killing site in Jalisco state
item.description: TEUCHITLAN, Mexico (AP) — When a group of citizens searching for missing relatives in the western state of Jalisco arrived at a remote ranch outside Mexico's second-largest city last week on an anonymous tip, all they had to do was push open the unlocked gate.
item.dc:creator: DIANA DE LA MORA and FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrian leader signs constitution that puts the country under an Islamist group's rule for 5 years
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria's interim president on Thursday signed a temporary constitution that leaves the country under Islamist rule while promising to protect the rights of all Syrians for five years during a transitional phase.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kuwait frees 10 jailed Americans, including contractors held on drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kuwait has released 10 American prisoners, including veterans and military contractors jailed for years on drug-related charges, in a move seen as a gesture of goodwill between two allies. “President Trump has made it a priority to bring home Americans detained overseas.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration resumes immigrant family detentions
item.description: The Trump administration has resumed family detention of immigrants, a legal nonprofit said, reigniting a controversial practice largely curtailed under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Asian elephant population in Cambodia is more robust than previously thought, a study finds
item.description: A genetic study of Asian elephants in northern Cambodia reveals a larger and more robust population than previously thought, raising hopes the endangered species could slowly recover.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Argentine retirees and soccer fans clash with police during protests against austerity measures
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Argentine retirees and fans of several soccer teams clashed with police Wednesday during a protest in front of Congress in the capital, Buenos Aires, against the economic policies implemented by the government of President Javier Milei.
item.dc:creator: SARA ESPAÑA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lutnick: Canada becoming US state 'best way' to merge economies
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Wednesday that Canada becoming a U.S. state is the “best way” to merge the two countries’ economies. “The best way, the president has said it, the best way to actually merge the economies of Canada and the United States is for Canada to become our 51st state.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Russia waiting for US-Ukraine cease-fire proposal details, Kremlin says
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters Wednesday that Russia must get the details on U.S.-Ukrainian cease-fire talks before commenting on the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 8 Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas says ceasefire talks resume
item.description: Israeli fire has killed eight Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and three more in the occupied West Bank over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said Tuesday, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza has largely held since late January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Canada suspends 25% electricity tariffs on US
item.description: This comes after what Ford said was a "productive" conversation with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: US lifts military freeze on Ukraine in push for 30-day ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. will immediately restore military aid and intelligence sharing to Ukraine following bilateral talks in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, on Tuesday, with Ukraine expressing "readiness" to accept a 30-day ceasefire proposal. "Ukraine expressed readiness to accept the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Evangelical leader says US must protect Syrian Christians from attacks by jihadi terrorists
item.description: A senior evangelical leader has called on the U.S. to not recognize new Syrian regime following bloodshed of religious minorities, including Christians and Alawites, carried out by radical Islamists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine launches biggest drone attack on Moscow, killing 2, as US talks begin
item.description: Ukraine launched its largest-ever drone attack on Moscow as a delegation met with U.S. officials in Saudi Arabia for talks about ending the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope gets good news from doctors: He's no longer in immediate danger
item.description: Doctors are still cautious and have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Greenland’s opposition party pushes closer US ties, independence from Denmark as Trump plays big in election
item.description: Greenland holds a parliamentary vote in an election that will likely reveal how the nation will seek independence from Denmark as well its response to President Trump's ambitions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte arrested at airport on ICC warrant for crime against humanity
item.description: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte was arrested at Manila Ninoy Aquino International Airport following an order from the International Criminal Court (ICC).
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US-Canada products are 99% tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump labels ex-Australian PM Malcolm Turnbull 'weak' amid spat
item.description: The president's attack on Turnbull came after comments Turnbull made to Bloomberg that Donald Trump's style is an "advantage" to China.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope Francis no longer in imminent danger due to pneumonia, but remains hospitalized
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors said Monday Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month, but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada braces for new leader: Here are Trudeau’s potential successors
item.description: Canada is bracing for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau’s successor, who will follow the Liberal Party leader that has led U.S.’s neighbor to the north since the Obama administration, The Associated Press reported. The change in leadership will come amid frosty U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon released from prison
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday, a day after a Seoul court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial for rebellion without being detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Aid efforts in Gaza slow as millions of promised USAID dollars do not arrive, agency officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Trump administration’s cuts to USAID have frozen hundreds of millions of dollars in contractual payments to aid groups, leaving them paying out of pocket to preserve a fragile ceasefire, according to officials from the U.S. humanitarian agency.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9
